description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 6 - Preparing for Sleep

tokens: What's the matter, aren't you feeling good?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I may go down and try to empty my bladder, here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm trying to figure out what we're supposed to be doing.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It's - Coming up on 8 o'clock, vent the batteries and terminate the cabin purge, you're supposed to do.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You're supposed to do another P52 IMU realign to REFSMMAT (garbled) You're supposed to do P52 IMU realign at about 08:20, about 25 minutes.
Want me to do that one, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'd better do that one of mine, and see what the optics look like.
Rusty, sit here and watch this, and make sure we don't get any gimbal lock.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, I'll tell you, I feel - I feel a heck of a lot better after eating.
I didn't really feel bad before eating, but I felt hungry.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, it does feel good after a hot lunch (garbled), doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, you know, swallowing all of this gas, you can't help but fart all over the place.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's a real challenge for that poor lithium hydroxide.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, you're kind of socking them in there, aren't you?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I'm going to try a small one, here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I'll have a couple of pieces of medium.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I can't figure out whether I'm sleepy.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Say, and Dave, there's a couple - You want some towels, too?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know how I'm going to get more - any more to eat, though.
I don't know but what I should go and eat one of those 'yuk' meals?
I wonder if I eat one of those things -
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, I have it over there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: There's our friendly O flow high.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, is that thing pulled out over there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Is that ECS return valve over there pulled out?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I suggest we turn off the LM tunnel vent.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's see which one is doing what.
Let's do them one at a time and look at the ECS.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's turn off the purge valve first.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're supposed to have that on, come to think about it.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm not sure that you're going to see any response, right away.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I got an O flow high (laughter).
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't even know what I did with that --
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's just leave it there for a while and see what it stabilizes out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Here, let me - Give me something.
Hey, Dave, watch out, you're going to get your fingers in your eyeball.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, here's a good way to hold your chow.
At least it seems to work pretty well.
You get a pair of (garbled) like this - Dave, let me have one of those
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, this is going to feel good, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You know how to get out that dump and take the (garbled)?
(garbled), I can't get the urine dump stopped.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're supposed to do a P52 or something, aren't we?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Jim, the flow is down to 0.6 and it's still going down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Would you believe we're looking right at the Earth?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's see, we're going to our right, so if David rolls left a couple of pulses, we'll be able to see it in a little while, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Do you know where I put - Where's my checklist?
If (garbled), I'm going to do this thing.
I've got to get my checklist.
Okay, what did I do with it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Singing) Hey, I feel like a new man.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'm going to get something to eat.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, you want me to get some of that good food out?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, would you get some of that (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I think I can reach it; let me try.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're not going to do a sextant calibration to the trunnion bias, are we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You're not going to do a sextant - sextant calibration to the trunnion bias, a P27 midcourse (garbled)?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: CMC - on; ISS - on; SCS - operating; BMAG (3); G&N Power optics; CMC Mode, Free.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We've got to get the screens and put on that one.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's - that, we want to happen.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Trouble is, you know when it comes back on and flips us off - flip on - off like that a couple of times, it's going to blow the horn.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, but it's not going to - it's going to stay on now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I know it; when it comes back up in the middle of the night, it'll - going to blow a horn.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, it'll only come to 235 [psi, 1620 kPa], see?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Now, what's it - what's it - what's the horn come on at?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got - I'm under the effect of this turning effect, here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Dave, don't these eyepieces move around?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got turkey and gravy out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Give me a - give me a pair of scissors, Dave, since you got them out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Is it okay to go to zero optics here, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I can't reach that, silly (garbled) What bag is that?
What's this bag - What's this floating here?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's my water bag up there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: There is nothing down there but Earth, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: If you roll left, I think - Well, I don't know, shoot.
Which way are we rolling - on the rate meter?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Where's all that equipment we dump the urine bags with?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, I'll look for that in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wonder what I'm going to do with that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, I've got to get our spoons out of there, too.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know where in the heck that - Yahoo!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know if they're going to stay there or not.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, I wonder if we're supposed to dump our waste water?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me see how high it's getting, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Waste is - waste is about 68 percent, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I'm about to dive in for them here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, we're still looking at the Earth, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (Singing) Have you got the utility light?
Hey, anybody got the utility light?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: You want the flashlight down here?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, I think I see them.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Boy, oh boy, these things weren't packed very well!
These spoons are - the bags of the spoons are all blown - are all blown up.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (Singing) Boy, this damn turning effect really grabs you, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're getting some stars out there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, Dave, doesn't that eyepiece slide around down there?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Then it turns around like this.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You made it through a docking this time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Dave, Just a little bit farther and I think I can get Rigel and Sirius.
You're going to have to come down here and show me how this thing works, tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: A spoon - I see a spoon.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, let me start through this dude.
Let's see, I want to call Verb 37; Verb 37, Enter; 52, Enter.
I want to do a REFSMMAT.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I guess I might as well throw them away.
You got the TSB over there, David?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see, I can come out of zero now, can't I?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You want Rigel and Sirius don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, then enter in 215; that's Sirius.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't you stop us now, Dave?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Shoot, I can't get the God-blessed thing in focus.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, I see him down there.
We do PICAPAR - we got that.
CMC verify; Optics zero - Off; Proceed - you hit a Proceed here, and then it goes and looks for it, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, that's it; now, put your shaft and trunnion out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm making up for what you guys were doing before, let me tell you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Do I go to manual here?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Boy, oh goodness gracious sakes alive!
They're going like a scalded ape.
Yes, we're really whipping along there, aren't we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me just see if I can catch it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Just see if he - he'll - he'll see if he can catch it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I just can't keep up with them.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, I guess I can't even stay up with it in - in LO.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, that looks like about enough.
I'm getting - I'm catching up with it now.
Optics - LO, will get it there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You open that new meal, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Was it a good mark, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, on those good marks I'll hit a Proceed; and that was a bright star, so hit a Proceed.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: That's behind the - oh, that's - behind the LM, I think.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: May not be behind the LM in the sextant.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, should I mark on it?
I can't possibly identify it as Sirius.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I have a feeling that when I open this bag that everything is going to fall in my face.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I read the wrong number, anyway.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: (garbled) Alright, and that one's the right star; I guess and (garbled) give it Auto optics.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Oh, shoot, damn, I screwed up!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, let's see, you want to go to the next - We want to get the torquing angles here, don't we?
Why don't you write those down on something or other?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We ought to get the big flight plan out.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Stand by, Jim; I'll get it for you now.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Where have you been writing yours down, Dave?
Where have you been writing yours down?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Now, you got to write the time, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That's the wrong - That's at 9 hours.
I'll just write it down in there.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: The time is 08:20 - Oh, you've got it down there, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, but you do it when you proceed, 08:24.
No, when the hell do you do it?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I do it - I did this one when I did the -
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You know, turkey and gravy is pretty good, but that other stuff will never hack it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, now what do we do with this one?
Enter; that ends it, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I got right through that, and didn't screw it up much.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, we'd better get this - this data figured out.
It's absolutely atrocious the way it is.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I am not reading you very good at all, but are you reading me well enough to take your block data.
I am ready to send that if you can read.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The block data, here I'll copy it - I got a - All I need to do is get rid of a few things here.
Where's the - You got that book?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And am I coming through all right, Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I've still got some more for you, but my first area was 009-3 Bravo.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: That is the end of the block data, and your SPS trim angles for this: pitch -133, yaw +135 and that is the end of the block data.
Before you start the readback, there are a couple of other comments for you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'd like to have you verify that you will do a waste water dump down to 25 percent prior to the rest period.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Waste water dump down to 25 percent prior to rest period.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That is affirmative, and we'd like to have a dosimeter reading, if you've got it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Here, I'll get mine; just a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: See if I can find mine, here.
Oh, shoot, there's the Hasselblad down there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're not going to be able to get his.
His dosimeter is all packed up.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And we've only got about 20 seconds here before we leave.
On this surge tank coming up, we say if you would bring the repress pack on the line and give us a reading on that, it might help us troubleshoot that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Send a repress back on the line!
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And we're going to lose you here, Apollo 9, at the end of the pass.
The next pass is scheduled over Hawaii at 05, which is right at the beginning of your rest period.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You want our fuel cell purge?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, we can dump the waste water.
Hey, you want to - What do we want to do with the block data?
We've got to figure out what to do with some of these.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, we've got to have a little council of war here when we - when we get through here.
Why don't you go ahead and eat, Dave?
I want to find a couple of other things.
I want to get the suit-hose thing.
We got to get that data figured out.
This is terrible, the way it's going around.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, we got too damn many books.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We've got to get the checklist out.
I'm going to start using that as the Bible.
I want to get this thing to dump my urine bag overboard, and I also want to get this thing to put on the vent line.
Hey, would you check, Rusty, and see how many hours of sleep we have tonight?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Sunrise coming up out at the front there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I guess we ought to stow the optics.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Put the dark slide back in the Hasselblad.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see, we want this on return line, don't we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm going to turn your suit flow back on now, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Let's see, the waste water is 70 percent.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: These hoses of mine are really keen.
These hoses of mine are really keen.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: What - what did you say, Dave?
Yes, I wouldn't doubt that at all.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Rusty, would you like a towel?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: No, not right at the moment.
I wouldn't quite know what to do with it.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Now, if I could just find - Urine bag, receiver, roll-on (garbled), waste management water in case of emergency - Whooee, I'm hot!
Do you know where that hose is, Dave?
Do you know where that hose is that we use to dump our suits?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Where's the contraption that goes on the thing?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's up - it's there right under my foot, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: - (laughter) on the flo - What in the heck is that?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let me get it hooked up here.
Do you remember how to do?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I guess it doesn't make any difference which of these two we plug it into; they just (garbled) together.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Let's just use - let's keep using the same one, though.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Which one are you going to use?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I'd like to use the one closest to the LEB.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You could start powering down to G&N if you want, Dave.
You could probably start powering down to G&N.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Feels like I have the bends in my knees.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I want to dump my urine bag, and I just went ahead and plugged it all (garbled) in there.
Let's see, we got to turn the heaters and stuff on, don't we?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, the urine dump heater's on.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, why don't we get the book out - the book of instructions out and read it to me?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Wait a minute, I'll just get the book of instructions out, and read it to myself.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'm about done eating here, I think.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: God, I've been eating for two rev's.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: This thing - this thing must have blown up my suit.
It blew up up (laughter) - my urine collection.
It must had 15 psi air trapped in it.
I plugged it in and it went oops!
Oh man, is that thing ever tight!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Just a second there, and I'll give you one more (garbled)
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I'll say one thing for this chow; it's tasty.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, Dave, where did you put my old towel?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Urine dump mode; here we go.
P and GA - PGA urine collection bag dump: connect urine transfer hose and filter to urine (garbled); connect urine transfer hose to thigh QD; waste management urine valve to dump.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Didn't even say anything about the heater, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hey, you want to start that powerdown, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, let me - let me just fold up this bag.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The circuit breakers and everything are ready for the urine dump.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, sir, let me verify them.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, I'm going to go ahead and dump this.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Hey, which one is it going to come out now, David?
You ought to - might as well watch it out there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I think it comes out your side over there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Right above the hatch - or right over here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Man, I can feel it gurgling like mad.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It's a nonthrust urine dump, huh?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't you take all your stuff when you get through with it and put in one big bag and wrap it up with tape, Rusty?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I already gave some to Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I thought he was putting it in - in over there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I put it back the food container over here, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We ought to wrap it in tape - you know.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: But we need to split - start splitting that tape in half.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I wonder how you know when this thing is empty.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Your dick goes out the door (laughter).
I shouldn't say that; we're being recorded, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't know, but when you're done, shoot; you can just pass it to me.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, and when you're done, you can pass it to me.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: By George, there's a LM out there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Urine is purging all over that.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, especially off the black stuff.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I ought to have a movie of this.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: It goes out - it will ricochet off of one - off of one surface and up to the other and bounce off the other.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: (Laughter) You know what it's like, it's like salt moving something.
Probably going to end up with urine in the thrusters.
By Jove, is that a hole?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Not sure you can see it.
Can you see the quad over here in front of me?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Do you see a piece of yellow tape there?
I don't think you can see it, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't think I can either.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't know - I can't tell whether it's little patch or whether it's a hole.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I don't know whether I'm sucked on or not.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You're still putting out lots of particles.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Why don't we go through that powerdown?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You know, there's one little particle out there that's spinning like mad.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Tell you what I'm going to do: I'm going to just pull this thing off of mine and let it purge through for a minute.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It won't purge, though, will it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: On the back of my checklist.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Who else wants to get plugged in?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here, why don't you just stick it right on me?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Oh, I can feel it going down.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Man, a lot more room in that thing now.
Okay, we ought to dump the water now, too, I guess, huh?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, give me about a minute.
Here, let me find it, Dave.
Cabin repress, command module pressure dump, bias, past urine water dump, potable chlorination, alignment, SPS.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Get Verb 48, Enter; hey, I think I'm empty.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Okay, load R-l, A equals zero, so I guess it says zero - anything.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Jim, have you seen this - this sun - on the DSKY?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Did it completely blank it out?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It re - it really wiped it out.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (garbled) spraying water out there like mad!
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: And it's not even hooked on you anymore.
Wonder how they'd do a purge on it now.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, it still doesn't have any purge fittings.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: It doesn't have to have any purge fittings.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: How do you purge the urine out of the line when they get done with it?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: There's another little fitting you put in there --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, but I wonder where it is.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Well, the last time I saw them, they were up underneath the couch here where I'm sitting.
No, there's a - there's a - an elbow somewhere down here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I think - I think it's right over here.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Yes, I think it's in the top one up there.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Well, it - No - Hey, Jim, it's in the back one over there, and it's in that - in that piece of - of stuff that came out.
There it is, on that wall.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Now I need to do all that.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just sucking it through there, Dave.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: I was cleaning it out, just to see if it was going to clean out.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Okay, Dave, you want to load a zero in the first slot.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Hold until the standby light's on.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, I was - I was turning my lights up, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Wait a minute; G&N Power IMU.
You ready to bite the bullet?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You mean if it's not one, we watch it grow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You've got to throw the switch, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: That does it; that zaps it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: G&N - It says it - I don't know --
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: No, it says G&N Power, IMU --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's see - That's the only one we got.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What are our rates right now, Dave?
I wonder if we ought to damp them down at all.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We got a little bit of yaw.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yes, it's always (garbled) FDAI Scale, 5 and 1.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Yes, I'm thinking, you know, we're - Here we go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me see if we're in the right thing here.

tokens: Okay, let's turn the jets off.

tokens: Yes, well, let me look at - on down.

tokens: Let's move that up, so we don't turn the jets on.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Move the 16 RCS jets off prior to going to rate command.
transcript.speaker: Scott

transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We're about 0.1 of a degree per second, Jim.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, why don't we just let it go?
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Limit cycle - on, of all things to have.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Rotation control power normal (2) to Off.

tokens: I thought that I heard some thing --
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Don't wiggle your feet around, Rusty.

tokens: Let me see what our fuel cells are doing now.

tokens: SCS Electric - SCS Electronic power - Off.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't remember it, but I - That's what I wondered --

tokens: Yes, I remember hearing that now.

tokens: We sure didn't drop much current.

tokens: That's all there is to the whole bloody powerdown checklist?

tokens: No, I turned that off right after I turned it on.

tokens: Yes, I'll get - I'll get that stuff in just a minute.

tokens: Why don't you get that dumped and over with so we can --

tokens: Oh, yes, then we got to start the waste water dump.

tokens: It would snap around the thing, Jim.

tokens: Where - where - where - where - where could it - that thing go?
